calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-24-2022 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-22-2022 Continued To Jun-15-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-01-2022 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
The following matters were heard concurrently:
CGC-19-579005 Anil Ravikanti vs. Twitter, Inc.
CGC-21-596299 Kumar Gupta Ravikanti Srinivas Anil vs. Twitter, Inc.
Mr. Ravikanti stated that he had a name change and therefore he is the same person in both cases.

Appearances:
Kumar Gupta Ravikanti (470.717.0811; ravikumarmr101@gmail.com) Plaintiff in propria persona.
Liz Sanchez Santiago, Esq. (415.693.2000; lsanchezsantiago@cooley.com) and Kyle Wong, Esq. (kwong@cooley.com) of Cooley LLP for Defendant via Zoom videoconference.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT TWITTER, INC.'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff's claim is barred by res judicata. Instructive is the case Long Beach Grand Prix Assn. v. Hunt (1994) 25 Cal.App.4th 1195, cited by Defendant in opposition. In Long Beach, the Court of Appeal stated, "[w]here a plaintiff enters a dismissal with prejudice as to a defendant there is obviously no due process problem with allowing the defendant to assert a plea of res judicata as to a subsequent action by the same plaintiff involving the same subject matter." (Id. at 1199.)

Regardless, Plaintiff again fails to state a cause of action. (Code of Civil Procedure, sec. 430.10(e).) Plaintiff alleges Defendant breached its Terms of Service ("TOS"), however the TOS expressly states that the Defendant can remove content and it disclaims all liability for the loss of data through the "Limitation of Liability" provision. (Sanchez Santiago Decl. Exh. B; See Order Sustaining Demurrer.) As the court previously stated, Defendant's limitation of liability is enforceable and controls. (Gen. Ins. Co. v. Truck Ins. Exchange (1966) 242 Cal.App.2d 419, 426; Lewis v. YouTube, LLC 244 Cal.App.4th 118; Order Sustaining Demurrer.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
